subtitle: When Names Fell From the Pot: Uttaramerur and the Law of Trust

panels.shot: epic low-angle wide of temple-front square at dusk
panels.action: Kudavolai Scribe of Uttaramerur stands before the stone walls of the Vaikunta Perumal temple, palm-leaf bundle in hand, as villagers gather around the open square in the warm, fading light of the Chola evening.

dialogue.text: In Uttaramerur, under Parantaka Chola, a village carved its own law of guardians into stone, and let the pot choose its servants.

i18n.hi: परांतक चोल के समय उत्तरमेरूर में गाँव ने अपने रखवालों का कानून पत्थर पर खुदा, और घड़े से अपने सेवक चुने।

panels.shot: wide aerial view of fertile village and temple complex
panels.action: From above, the fields, irrigation channels, tiled roofs and the central Vaikunta Perumal temple of Uttaramerur spread out in ordered lines, with smoke rising from hearths and people moving like threads through the streets.

dialogue.text: This was Uttaramerur in the early tenth century, a Chola brahmadeya village of wet fields, sacred groves and stone temples, tied by tax and duty to the tiger-banner throne.

i18n.hi: यह था दसवीं सदी की शुरुआत का उत्तरमेरूर, चोलों का एक ब्रह्मदेय गाँव—सींची हुई खेतियाँ, पवित्र उपवन, पत्थर के मंदिर, और कर व कर्तव्य से बाघ–ध्वज सिंहासन से बँधा।

panels.shot: medium shot of Kudavolai Scribe seated under a tree with palm leaves
panels.action: Kudavolai Scribe of Uttaramerur sits cross-legged under a shady tree near the temple, stylus poised over palm leaves, watching villagers argue and gesture about village matters in the background.

dialogue.text: Land taxes, irrigation, temple offerings, the repair of bunds—every decision of the sabha needed men who would guard the village welfare and the king’s revenue with steady hands.

i18n.hi: जमीन के कर, सिंचाई, मंदिर की नৈवेद्य, बंध की मरम्मत—सभा के हर फैसले को ऐसे लोग चाहिए थे जो गाँव की भलाई और राजा की आमदनी दोनों की चौकसी करें।

panels.shot: slow push-in on the scribe’s face, intent and thoughtful
panels.action: Kudavolai Scribe of Uttaramerur looks up from his leaf, brows knit in concentration, as elders speak urgently before him, their hands tracing circles in the dust.

dialogue.text: Under Parantaka I, the sabha of Uttaramerur had already long governed itself, but now it sought to bind its customs in clear words, for all and for later kings to see.

i18n.hi: परांतक पहला के राज में उत्तरमेरूर की सभा पहले से ही स्वयं शासन करती थी, पर अब वह चाहती थी कि उसकी रीति साफ शब्दों में बँध जाए, सबके लिए और आने वाले राजाओं के लिए।

panels.shot: wide shot of temple mandapa filled with seated brahmin villagers
panels.action: Inside the stone mandapa of the Vaikunta Perumal temple, rows of brahmin villagers sit in ordered lines, some with their hands on their knees, others holding palm-leaf bundles; lamps flicker against carved pillars.

dialogue.text: The sabha met in the temple hall, a body of landholding brahmins, the urar of the brahmadeya, who would now set down rules for choosing the wardens of tank, field and shrine.

i18n.hi: सभा मंदिर के मंडप में जुटी—जमींदार ब्राह्मणों का जत्था, ब्रह्मदेय के उरार—जो अब सरोवर, खेत और देवालय के रखवालों की चयन–मर्यादा तय करने वाले थे।

panels.shot: medium shot of elder standing, addressing assembly
panels.action: An elderly brahmin elder, white hair tied back, stands among the seated men, one hand raised as he speaks; Kudavolai Scribe of Uttaramerur sits to one side, listening intently with stylus ready.

dialogue.speaker: chola_local_self_governance
dialogue.text: If we choose carelessly, we ruin both the king’s trust and our own.

i18n.hi: अगर हमने ढिलाई से चुना, तो राजा का भरोसा भी टूटेगा, हमारा भी।

panels.shot: close-up of stylus writing on palm leaf
panels.action: A close view shows Kudavolai Scribe of Uttaramerur’s hand as he carves precise Tamil letters into a fresh palm leaf, the grain of the leaf catching the light.

dialogue.text: They spoke of age, of learning, of property, of prior service. The scribe caught their words, shaping them into firm demands on those who wished to guard the village.

i18n.hi: उन्होंने उम्र, विद्या, संपत्ति और पिछली सेवा की बात की। लिपिक ने उनकी बातें पकड़ीं, उन्हें कड़े नियमों में ढाला, जो गाँव के रखवालों पर लागू होंगे।

panels.shot: medium shot of younger man listening anxiously at door
panels.action: A young landholder in simple white cloth stands at the edge of the mandapa, half in shadow, watching the assembly with hopeful yet uneasy eyes, fingers nervously gripping his waistcloth.

dialogue.text: Not all could serve. The rules named only men between thirty-five and seventy, with land of their own and knowledge of accounts and of mantras, steady in mind and body.

i18n.hi: हर कोई सेवा के योग्य नहीं था। नियमों ने सिर्फ पैंतीस से सत्तर वर्ष के उन पुरुषों को माना, जिनकी अपनी जमीन हो, हिसाब–किताब और मंत्रों की समझ हो, और मन–तन स्थिर हो।

panels.shot: wide shot showing elders murmuring as scribe reads list of disqualifications
panels.action: Kudavolai Scribe of Uttaramerur stands now, palm leaf held before him, reading aloud as elders murmur, some nodding, some avoiding each other’s eyes; a few men in the background exchange uneasy glances.

dialogue.text: They were stricter about those barred: men who had misused temple land, failed to render accounts, taken bribes, drunk heavily, or had close kin already on the committee, would not be trusted with new office.

i18n.hi: पर मनाही के बारे में वे और भी सख्त थे: जिसने मंदिर की जमीन का दुरुपयोग किया, हिसाब न दिया, घूस ली, मद्यपान में डूबा, या जिसकी निकट की रिश्तेदारी पहले से सभा में हो, उस पर नया पद भरोसे लायक नहीं था।

panels.shot: close-up on the troubled face of a disqualified man
panels.action: A middle-aged villager, features tense, looks downward as he hears the conditions; a distant recollection of an unpaid temple due weighs on his expression.

dialogue.text: One misdeed could stain not only the man, but his house; the inscription barred whole families if one member had brought shame to public duty.

i18n.hi: एक गलती से केवल वह आदमी नहीं, उसका घर भी दागदार होता; शिलालेख ने पूरे कुलों को रोका, अगर एक सदस्य ने सार्वजनिक सेवा को कलंकित किया हो।

panels.shot: low-angle shot of scribe indicating temple wall where inscription will go
panels.action: Kudavolai Scribe of Uttaramerur points toward a smooth section of the temple’s outer wall, stone masons nearby holding chisels and hammers, as elders discuss where the new text shall be carved.

dialogue.text: This was not to be a secret law. They chose the temple wall as their witness, that any who read it would know how Uttaramerur selected its guardians.

i18n.hi: यह कोई छुपा हुआ कानून नहीं था। उन्होंने मंदिर की दीवार को साक्षी चुना, ताकि जो भी पढ़े, जान सके कि उत्तरमेरूर अपने रखवालों को कैसे चुनता है।

panels.shot: close-up of stone mason chiseling first Tamil letters
panels.action: A stone mason leans close to the granite, hammer poised, chisel biting as the first bold Tamil characters appear sharply on the dark surface.

dialogue.text: Year twelve of Parantaka, they recorded, under the tiger king’s regnal count, that these were the kudavolai rules for the wardens of Uttaramerur Chaturvedimangalam.

i18n.hi: उन्होंने लिखा: परांतक के राज के बारहवें वर्ष में, बाघ–राजा की राजगिनती के अंतर्गत, ये हैं उत्तरमेरूर चतुर्वेदीमंगलम के वार्डनों के लिए कूडवोलै के नियम।

panels.shot: wide shot of sabha preparing for kudavolai drawing in open square
panels.action: In the open square outside the temple, men arrange low platforms; a large polished pot sits at the center, with palm-leaf slips stacked neatly beside it, while villagers gather in a ring.

dialogue.text: Now came the heart of their device: the kudavolai, the pot-ticket, by which chance, bound by rules, would choose the members of the village committees.

i18n.hi: अब आया उनके उपाय का हृदय: कूडवोलै, यानी घड़ा–परची, जिसमें नियमों से बँधा संयोग गाँव की समितियों के सदस्यों को चुनता था।

panels.shot: medium shot of scribe writing names on palm slips
panels.action: Kudavolai Scribe of Uttaramerur bends over a low wooden plank, writing candidate names on narrow palm slips, while another elder checks each name against a list.

dialogue.text: For each ward, they wrote the names of those who met every test—on property, age, learning, and conduct—no more, no less; their names alone would go into the pot.

i18n.hi: हर वार्ड के लिए उन्होंने उन सब के नाम लिखे जो हर कसौटी पर खरे उतरते थे—जमीन, उम्र, विद्या और चालचलन—न ज्यादा, न कम; बस उन्हीं के नाम घड़े में जाने थे।

panels.shot: close-up of hand folding palm slip and dropping into pot
panels.action: A close view shows Kudavolai Scribe of Uttaramerur’s hand carefully folding a narrow palm slip and dropping it into the earthen pot, where other folded slips already lie within.

dialogue.text: Folded so that no mark showed, the slips fell into the pot. Names became identical leaves, stripped of rank and wealth, before the draw.

i18n.hi: परचियों को इस तरह मोड़ा कि कोई निशान न दिखे, और वे घड़े में गिर पड़ीं। नाम पत्तों जैसे एक–से हो गए, ओहदे और दौलत से खाली, चिट्ठी निकलने से पहले।

panels.shot: wide shot of assembly as young boy is led to pot to draw
panels.action: In the center of the gathered sabha, a small boy with shaved head and tuft walks hesitantly toward the pot, guided by an elder; Kudavolai Scribe of Uttaramerur watches, palm leaf ready to record.

dialogue.text: They chose a child to draw the slips, a hand untainted by faction. Before all eyes, he would reach in and let fate, under law, appoint the guardians.

i18n.hi: उन्होंने परचियाँ निकालने को एक बच्चे को चुना—ऐसा हाथ जो गुटबाज़ी से अछूता हो। सबकी आँखों के सामने वही हाथ घड़े में जाएगा, और विधि, कानून के भीतर, रखवालों को चुनेगी।

panels.shot: close-up of the boy’s hand inside the pot, fingers brushing slips
panels.action: The frame tightens on the boy’s small hand plunged into the dark mouth of the pot, his fingers brushing against the smooth folded slips inside.

dialogue.text: A hush fell. In the cool clay darkness, the child’s fingers moved among the leaves, while men who had ploughed and recited for decades held their breath.

i18n.hi: सन्नाटा छा गया। ठंडी मिट्टी के अँधेरे में बच्चे की उँगलियाँ पत्तों के बीच घूमीं, और दशकों से हल चलाने और वेद पढ़ने वाले पुरुषों ने सांस रोक ली।

panels.shot: medium shot of boy handing slip to scribe, assembly leaning in
panels.action: The boy holds out a chosen slip to Kudavolai Scribe of Uttaramerur, who takes it solemnly, while the circle of villagers leans forward in tense anticipation.

dialogue.speaker: chola_local_self_governance
dialogue.text: For the garden land committee of this ward, the pot chooses…

i18n.hi: इस वार्ड की बाग़–जमीन समिति के लिए, घड़ा चुनता है…

panels.shot: close-up of scribe reading name from slip, face calm
panels.action: Kudavolai Scribe of Uttaramerur unfolds the slip and reads, his face kept deliberately calm, eyes reflecting the weight of the moment.

dialogue.text: The inscription listed each standing committee, each number of seats, and recorded that for each, the names thus drawn must serve, unless some hidden disqualification were later found.

i18n.hi: शिलालेख ने हर स्थायी समिति, उसकी सीटों की संख्या बताई, और दर्ज किया कि जिन-जिन नामों की परची निकले, वे ही सेवा करेंगे—जब तक बाद में कोई छुपी कमी न पकड़ी जाए।

panels.shot: wide shot of chosen men stepping forward modestly
panels.action: Several men, each from different wards, step forward from the ring with modest demeanour, hands joined, while others watch them with a mix of respect and relief; the temple rises behind them.

dialogue.text: Those chosen would serve for a set term, then step down. The rules forbade the same man to be reappointed too soon, so that many would taste both honour and responsibility.

i18n.hi: चुने गए लोग एक ठहराए हुए कार्यकाल तक सेवा करेंगे, फिर हट जाएँगे। नियमों ने मना किया कि वही व्यक्ति जल्दी फिर से न चुना जाए, ताकि कई लोग सम्मान और जिम्मेदारी दोनों का स्वाद चख सकें।

panels.shot: medium shot of scribe and elder conferring over palm leaf regarding removal rules
panels.action: Kudavolai Scribe of Uttaramerur and a senior elder sit together, heads close over a palm leaf, as they discuss and mark lines setting out how a negligent or corrupt member might be removed.

dialogue.text: They planned also for failure. If a member withheld accounts, embezzled or avoided meetings, the sabha could remove him and bar him from future service, protecting the committees from rot.

i18n.hi: उन्होंने असफलता के लिए भी प्रबंध किया। जो सदस्य हिसाब रोके, धन हड़पे या बैठकों से कत्तराए, उसे सभा हटा सकती थी और आगे की सेवा से रोक सकती थी, ताकि समितियाँ अंदर से न सड़ें।

panels.shot: wide shot of stone inscription panel nearly complete on temple wall
panels.action: On the outer wall, a large granite panel now almost entirely covered with fine Tamil script stands in sharp relief, masons wiping stone dust as villagers look on with quiet pride.

dialogue.text: Two years later, in Parantaka’s fourteenth year, they added further clauses, refining procedure, tightening rules on attendance, and writing still more of their practice into stone.

i18n.hi: दो साल बाद, परांतक के चौदहवें वर्ष में, उन्होंने और धाराएँ जोड़ीं—प्रक्रिया और साफ की, हाज़िरी के नियम कसे, और अपनी और प्रथाएँ पत्थर पर उतारीं।

panels.shot: close-up of weathered inscription centuries later
panels.action: Centuries later, the same stone inscription is seen worn but legible, its letters softened by time, lichen creeping along the edges, a faint touch tracing the old script.

dialogue.text: A thousand harvests have come and gone, kings have fallen, canals have shifted, yet the letters of Uttaramerur still speak of a village that bound its own power with rules and trusted the pot to choose its guardians.

i18n.hi: हजार फसलें कट चुकीं, राजा गिर चुके, नहरें रास्ता बदल चुकीं, पर उत्तरमेरूर के अक्षर अब भी बोलते हैं—उस गाँव की, जिसने अपने अधिकार को नियमों से बाँधा और घड़े पर भरोसा किया कि वह उसके रखवालों को चुनेगा।

title_i18n.en: The Chola Village Chooses Its Guardians
title_i18n.hi: चोल गाँव ने अपने रखवालों को चुना

subtitle_i18n.en: When Names Fell From the Pot: Uttaramerur and the Law of Trust
subtitle_i18n.hi: जब घड़े से गिरे नाम: उत्तरमेरूर और भरोसे का विधान
